name: Cabracan
description: Cabracan, also known as Earthquake, was a powerful giant and demon in Mayan mythology. He was the son of Vucub Caquix, a boastful and arrogant bird deity, and Chimalmat. Cabracan was renowned for his immense strength and his ability to destroy mountains with a stomp of his foot. He was a destructive force of nature, embodying the raw power of earthquakes and volcanic eruptions. His primary role was to topple mountains and shake the earth, causing chaos and destruction wherever he went. The Hero Twins, Hunahpu and Xbalanque, eventually sought to defeat Cabracan and his brother Zipacna to restore balance to the world. They tricked Cabracan into eating poisoned birds covered in earth, weakening him significantly. Once weakened, the Hero Twins were able to bind and imprison him, thus ending his reign of terror and preventing further devastation of the Mayan landscape. Cabracan represents the untamed forces of nature and the constant struggle between order and chaos in the Mayan worldview. His defeat symbolizes the triumph of cleverness and strategy over brute strength.
subtitle: Earthquake Demon and Mountain Destroyer

keyMyths.title: The Destruction of Mountains
keyMyths.description: Cabracan used his immense strength to topple mountains with his feet, causing widespread destruction and terror.

keyMyths.title: The Trickery of the Hero Twins
keyMyths.description: Hunahpu and Xbalanque poisoned birds with earth and tricked Cabracan into eating them, weakening him for capture.

keyMyths.title: Imprisonment by the Hero Twins
keyMyths.description: After being weakened, Cabracan was bound and imprisoned by Hunahpu and Xbalanque, ending his destructive reign.
